user_message: I need to prep a brief for the Litware Inc engagement.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 10:01 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Litware Inc pipeline hygiene

Just checking in on the pipeline hygiene proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 13:26 EST
Subject: Re: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-01-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft), Kevin (Litware Inc)

[00:02:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:06:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:08:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:12:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:17:00] Kevin (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:20:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:27:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:29:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:31:00] Kevin (Litware Inc): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
